segments.name: formation_intro
segments.text: The offense lines up in the 58/59 Under alignment for a Pass Protection play. This is a 2 wide receiver, 1 tight end set with both a running back and fullback in the backfield. Formation strength is to the left.

segments.name: snap
segments.text: The center delivers a clean snap to the quarterback to initiate the pass protection play.

segments.name: protection
segments.text: The offensive line executes the protection scheme — each lineman and the TE takes their assigned defender.

segments.name: te_route
segments.text: The tight end initially executes a reach block on the strong edge to seal the outside. If the initial defender is accounted for, the TE can release into an intermediate seam/vertical as a secondary passing option.

segments.name: fb_route
segments.text: The fullback performs a protection check — identifying any delayed blitzers, engaging immediate threats, then settling into the flat as a safety-valve option if the QB needs a quick outlet.

segments.name: wr_x_route
segments.text: Wide receiver X runs a vertical stem (deep route) to stretch the defense and act as the primary downfield read. His vertical threat pulls safety attention and creates space underneath for other routes.

segments.name: wr_z_route
segments.text: Wide receiver Z runs an intermediate dig/crossing route, working to the soft spot in zone coverage at about 12–15 yards and serving as the secondary primary passing option.

segments.name: rb_route
segments.text: The running back executes a protection assignment — chip or pick up a blitz, then settle into the flat as the primary checkdown option to provide the QB a safe outlet.

segments.name: qb_dropback
segments.text: The quarterback takes a short drop and sets in the pocket, scanning his progression while trusting the protection. He times his throws to the primary vertical and intermediate reads.

segments.name: passing_primary
segments.text: The primary reads are the two outside receivers — first WR X on the deep vertical, then WR Z on the intermediate dig. If either is open the QB takes the shot; if covered he looks to the running back or fullback in the flat as a quick checkdown. The protection scheme is designed to prioritize giving time for these reads.
